key_insight: A symmetric cryogenic piezo strain cell can deliver homogeneous uniaxial strain to thick, square quantum-device chips while minimizing flexure, shear, and electrical interference.
engineering_relevance: This design makes strain tuning practical for semiconductor qubits by supporting dense RF/DC wiring, preserving electrical isolation, and improving compatibility with device-style substrates.
method: Dual-chip symmetric loading to suppress bending and shear
High-density RF/DC interposer for standard wire bonding
Grounded Faraday cage around piezo actuators to reduce Stark-shift risk
evidence_signal: Finite element simulations and surface strain measurements on a 200 μm silicon die validated the design, with 215 μϵ achieved under 200 V piezo bias.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful alignment and electrical isolation; dense packaging and cryogenic integration may still introduce mechanical or wiring-related constraints.
paper_title: A Cryogenic Uniaxial Strain Cell for Quantum Devices
